introduction.text: Welcome back! Today we start with Basic Strategy. It's a map that tells you exactly what to do. We'll start with 'Hard Hands' (hands without an Ace). Look at the edges of the table: when you have very little, you take. When you have a lot, you wait. Simple, right? Let's prove it!

scenarios.title: Safe Zone (Total 8)
scenarios.goal: Show that hitting on 8 or less is risk-free.

sequence.text: You have 8 points. Many players are afraid to hit here, but that's a mistake. The highest card is 10. 8 + 10 = 18. You cannot Bust. Always HIT!

finish.text: See? You turned a weak 8 into a solid 17. I had 16, so I had to hit and I busted with 26. Safe win.

scenarios.title: Desperation (Total 5)
scenarios.goal: Reinforce hitting on very low hands against strong dealers.

sequence.text: You have 5 points. That's basically nothing. The dealer shows an Ace - a monster. No time for fancy moves. You just need points. Click HIT.

finish.text: You got a Queen and now have 15. I have 20. You lost, but you played correctly. You can't win every hand, but you must play right.

scenarios.title: Hard 17 vs Ace (The Trap)
scenarios.goal: Teach the player NEVER to hit on Hard 17.

sequence.text: Warning! This is a trap. You have 17. Dealer has an Ace. Your gut says 'Hit', but Math says 'Stand'. Hitting 17 is suicide. Never do it.

finish.text: You lost (17 vs 18), but... you played correctly! If you had hit, you would likely have busted. In Blackjack, a good decision doesn't always mean a win.

sequence.text: Two face cards! You have 20. Second best hand in the game. Nothing to think about here. Wait for the payout.

finish.text: Easy money. I have 19, you have 20. You win.

sequence.text: You have 18. Dealer has 9 (looks strong). Tempted to hit? Don't. With 18, the risk of busting is huge. Trust the math.

finish.text: Dealer Bust! See? I had 15 (9+6), so I had to hit and caught a 10. If you had panicked, you might have lost against yourself.

conclusion.text: You've mastered the edges of the table. Rule: 5-8 always HIT, 17-21 always STAND. The problems start in the middle... In the next lesson, I will teach you how to be aggressive and when to Double Down!
